beats.activity: Wakes up, coffee, checks phone and weather forecast
beats.reasoning: Morning routine at home in Eastlake; yesterday's moderate energy (45/100) and rainy forecast mean Ellie wakes at a reasonable hour but isn't rushing—drizzle sets a slower Tuesday mood.

beats.activity: Drives to studio, avoids SR-520 eastbound closure by taking I-5 through downtown
beats.reasoning: Commute to Fremont studio (actually located on First Hill per profile); SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon forces Ellie to plan around it by leaving early via I-5 to avoid the snarl and drizzle.

beats.activity: Opens studio, checks client bookings, preps stations and sterilizes equipment; Tattoo consultation and first session with a new client
beats.reasoning: Early arrival allows Ellie to settle into the studio before the day's rain peaks; meticulous nature means she values a clean, organized workspace to start the day right.

beats.activity: Quick lunch break at a nearby café, escapes the rain indoors
beats.reasoning: Rainy day means Ellie chooses an indoor meal spot close to the studio; her adventurous eating style and love of Capitol Hill cafés make a short drive worth it for a good lunch.

beats.activity: Drives back to studio through rain
beats.reasoning: Rain continues steady until 2pm; short commute back to work after lunch.

beats.activity: Afternoon tattoo sessions with regular clients
beats.reasoning: Solid afternoon of work; rain eases by 2pm, but Ellie stays focused on back-to-back sessions—yesterday's balance means today is more work-focused to maintain studio momentum.

beats.activity: Drives home through clearing weather, stops at Whole Foods for groceries and meal prep
beats.reasoning: Rain clearing by late afternoon; Ellie's meticulous nature and planning mean she shops near home for the week; stops on the way back to Eastlake.

beats.activity: Arrives home, unpacks groceries, makes dinner
beats.reasoning: Back at home in Eastlake; Ellie unwinds with meal prep and cooking—her adventurous eating style means she enjoys planning and preparing food.

beats.activity: Eats dinner, scrolls thrift sites and emails with Maya about weekend camping plans; Winds down with a show, updates expense spreadsheet, prepares for bed
beats.reasoning: Leisure evening at home; Ellie values close friendships and loves planning outdoor adventures—spending time researching camping spots and chatting with Maya aligns with her personality.
Full 8 hours of sleep ending at 6am; rainy, lower-energy day means Ellie gets solid rest to recharge for the week ahead.

diary:
Woke to that grey Seattle drizzle—the kind that makes you grateful for a hot cup of coffee before facing the day. The SR-520 closure had everyone scrambling, so I took I-5 through downtown instead. Studio was quiet this morning, which felt good. Prepped everything methodically, then met with a new client for a consultation and first session. There's always something special about starting fresh ink on someone.

Lunch at Delancey was a lifesaver—just me, a sandwich, and the smell of wet pavement outside. The rain kept coming down, so the drive back felt meditative somehow. Afternoon was steady: regulars came in, needles hummed, stories got shared across the chair. By the time I left, the clouds were breaking up. Whole Foods run felt productive. At home now with groceries unpacked, dinner made, and Maya texting about camping spots for the weekend. Scrolling thrift sites with a glass of wine, feeling that particular tiredness that comes from good, focused work.
thought_process: The weather dictated my route this morning—I didn't even hesitate about avoiding the bridge closure; that's just practical thinking in this city. I stayed focused on work because rainy days always feel busier in the studio, like people want to be somewhere warm and purposeful. The new client consultation energized me; after twenty years, that still matters. I took the lunch break deliberately, knowing I needed to step outside the studio even if I was stepping into rain. The afternoon sessions felt natural, rhythmic. By evening, my mood had lifted with the clouds—I'm genuinely excited about the camping trip, and handling the spreadsheet felt like closing a satisfying loop on the day. Rain days often make me introspective but also grounded; they remind me I like the routine I've built.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
